Example: A Nike instagram ad that ran all throughout the month of May costed $4,000.

Definition: How many folks are click on your ad
Example: If I click on an Instagram ad, this counts as a click

Definition: How many folks are viewing your ad
Example: If I saw an Instagram ad, this counts as an impression

Definition: Click-through-rate (The ratio between Clicks to Impressions)
Example: If there are 2 clicks in an ad that generated 10 impressions, the CTR would be 20%.

Definition: Cost per click (The ratio between how much the ad cost and how many clicks it generated)
Example: A Nike instagram ad that ran all throughout the month of May costed $4,000 and had 400 clicks. The CPC (Cost per click) was $10.

Definition: Conversions are actions. Actions can be sales, email sign ups, applications, and etc.
Example: If I saw an Instagram ad, and clicked on the ad, and bought something from their website, this would count as a conversion.

Definition: Conversion per click. (The ratio between how many conversions (actions) are to clicks.
Example: A Nike Instagram ad resulted in 100 clicks, and out of the 10 clicks, there were 10 conversions/purchases, it had a CPA of $10.

Definition: Cost per action. (The ratio between how many conversions (actions)
Example: A Nike Instagram ad cost $100 in the end of the campaign, it had $5 sales resulting from the ad, the CPA is $20. It cost $20 to have someone make a conversion.

Definition: Return on Investment (The ratio between revenue generated to cost)
Example: A Nike Instagram ad made $100 in revenue, the cost of the ad was $10, it has a ROI of $10.
